rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical cascade cyclization of 2-aryl-N-acryloyl indoles with arylsulfonyl hydrazides (or KBr) to access sulfonyl- and bromo-substituted indolo[2,1-a]isoquinolinones via iodide/bromide mediation.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (or bromide) to molecular I2 (or Br radical); I2 then activates arylsulfonyl hydrazide to release arylsulfonyl radical; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: molecular iodine (I2) generated by anodic oxidation of 2 I-
C-centered radical B from sulfonyl radical addition to acryloyl alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical C from 6-endo-trig cyclization onto 2-aryl ring
arenium cation D after oxidation of C (by anode or I2)

electron_flow: At the anode, two iodide anions lose 2 e- to form I2, which converts arylsulfonyl hydrazide into Ts-I and releases a sulfonyl radical; this radical adds to the acrylamide olefin, undergoes 6-endo-trig cyclization onto the 2-aryl ring, and the resulting cyclohexadienyl radical is further oxidized (anodically or by I2) to a cation that deprotonates to give the aromatic product, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully inhibited, indicating a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: PBN (N-tert-butyl-α-phenylnitrone)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully inhibited under standard conditions.

probe_reagents.name: I2 (stoichiometric, no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a formed in 48% yield without electricity, supporting I2 as the active mediator.

probe_reagents.name: NIS
probe_reagents.observation: 3a formed in 45% yield without current, consistent with electrophilic iodine species mediating the reaction.

probe_reagents.name: Ts-I (4-methylbenzenesulfonyl iodide)
probe_reagents.observation: 3a formed in 41% yield without current, supporting Ts-I as an on-cycle intermediate.

notes: X-ray structure (CCDC 2050276) confirms product connectivity for 4a. For the bromo variant, an analogous cycle is proposed in which a Br radical is generated anodically from KBr and adds to the alkene to initiate the same 6-endo-trig cascade. No KIE, CV, EPR, or DFT data were reported.
